prompt: A 17th-century copperplate scientific engraving of a dramatic mountainous terrain shown in cross-section, in the style of a Age of Exploration field atlas. The landscape is rendered entirely in fine cross-hatching and stippling with no photographic shading. A single winding path descends from a high peak, zigzagging along the steepest slopes downward through ridges and saddle points, converging on the deepest valley basin at the centre. Small arrow marks along the path indicate direction of descent. Several other paths from surrounding peaks all trend downward toward the same basin. Aged cream parchment paper with foxing and plate marks. Compass rose in corner. No color, pure black ink linework only. No modern elements.
tradition_name: Scientific engraving from the Age of Exploration
tradition_approach: Copperplate cross-hatching and stippling for all shading, topographic cross-section composition showing elevation, aged parchment with foxing, compass rose marginalia — targeting linework precision and cartographic spatial logic.
